column2: Provide and maintain reliable, secure, and rapid communications based on wartime requirements to support Naval Operating Forces.

column1: Naval communications must satisfy what systems?
column2: Defense Communications System (DCS) and National Communications System (NCS).
All information systems using electric or electromagnetic signals to transmit information between or among points.
All end terminal processing equipment, transmission, switching, cryptographic, and control devices used to transmit operational information in the Navy.
Unclassified but Sensitive Internet Protocol Router Network.
Navy Computer and Telecommunications Area Master Station.
Mandatory drastic reduction of normal message and telephone traffic so vital traffic is not delayed.
Chief of Naval Operations (CNO), fleet commanders in chief, and area coordinators.
I am busy; please do not interfere.
Specifies circuits/channels/facilities and policies/procedures; assigns comm tasks.
Report critical information to senior government decision makers (the President).
Authority in whose name message is sent; presumed to be the CO.
Designated individual authorized to release messages for transmission.
Caveat; RD; FRD; LIMDIS; FOUO; EFTO; SPECAT; PERSONAL FOR; NATO RESTRICTED; ALLIED RESTRICTED.
Most widely used DoD message format; usually UNCLASSIFIED but can be up to TOP SECRET.
Subject line starts with SUBJ/ and ends with //.
Remarks section explains the purpose of the message.
End of a message (after 8 blank lines).
